channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Secretary of State Blinken is returning to the Mideast in his latest diplomatic foray
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken is returning to the Middle East this week on his 12th visit since the Israel-Hamas war erupted last year but his first since the ouster of Syrian President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mega Millions winner sues for other half of $394M jackpot
item.description: A man is suing the California Lottery, claiming he bought both winning tickets for a $394 million Mega Millions jackpot but lost one and is owed the remaining $197 million.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: FIFA names Saudi Arabia as 2034 World Cup host; Spain, Portugal and Morocco to co-host 2030 edition
item.description: ZURICH (AP) — Saudi Arabia has been officially confirmed by FIFA as host of the 2034 World Cup in men’s soccer. The Saudi bid was the only candidate and was acclaimed by the applause of more than 200 FIFA member federations.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bundles of meth found inside fuel tank of SUV near border
item.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection officers seized 68 pounds of methamphetamine hidden inside a fuel tank.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Luna, NewsNation

item.title: The burial site of the people Andrew Jackson enslaved was lost. The Hermitage says it is found
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — At least 26 enslaved people died on the Tennessee plantation of President Andrew Jackson between 1804 and the end of the Civil War in 1865. Where they were laid to rest is knowledge that had been lost to time.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Sanders says this will likely be his last term
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.), one of the Senate’s most prominent progressives, says he likely won’t run again for the Senate after his fourth term expires in 2030.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nancy Mace allegedly attacked on Capitol Hill; suspect arrested
item.description: Mace posted about the incident on X, saying, "One new brace for my wrist and some ice for my arm, and it’ll heal just fine.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Malibu wildfire prompts evacuation orders and warnings for 20,000, including Dick Van Dyke and Cher
item.description: Weather conditions are forecast to improve this week in Southern California and aid firefighters in their battle against a wildfire that’s forced up to 20,000 people from their homes.
item.dc:creator: Jae C. Hong, Eric Thayer And Stefanie Dazio, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO shouts on way into courthouse and fights extradition
item.description: The man charged with murder in the killing of the CEO of UnitedHealthcare has made it clear he isn't going to make things easy for authorities.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mike Huckabee 'commends' Biden support for Israel's self-defense
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin kayaker who faked his death and fled to Eastern Europe is in custody, online records show
item.description: Online records show a Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning and left behind a wife and three children to go to Eastern Europe is in a Wisconsin jail.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Oregon federal judge temporarily blocks proposed Kroger-Albertsons merger
item.description: Oregon was one of several states to join the Federal Trade Commission’s lawsuit that aimed to block the merger.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, Fox 5

item.title: San Diego sheriff defies new policy to limit cooperation with immigration officials
item.description: The sheriff of the nation’s fifth-largest county on Tuesday defied a new policy to limit cooperation with federal immigration authorities, setting up a showdown over a new obstacle to President-elect Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans.
item.dc:creator: Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Joe Burrow's home broken into during Monday Night Football
item.description: Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow's home was broken into during Monday Night Football in the latest home invasion of a pro athlete in the U.S., authorities said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Key details about the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The 26-year-old man charged in last week’s killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO appeared in a Pennsylvania courtroom Tuesday, where he was denied bail and his lawyer said he'd fight extradition to New York City, where the attack happened. Luigi Nicholas Mangione was arrested Monday in the Dec.
item.dc:creator: SARAH BRUMFIELD and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McDonald's in Pennsylvania slammed with bad reviews after arrest of Luigi Mangione
item.description: A McDonald's in Altoona, Pennsylvania, is getting backlash online after an employee called 911, leading to the arrest of Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, KTLA 5

item.title: A fugitive gains fame in New Orleans eluding dart guns and nets
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A scruffy little fugitive is on the lam again in New Orleans, gaining fame as he outwits a tenacious band of citizens armed with night-vision binoculars, nets and a tranquilizer rifle.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mangione family in Maryland: What we know
item.description: Although many people outside of Maryland came to know the "Mangione" name only after the arrest of Luigi Mangione, 26, who is accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, the family has a number of prominent ties to the state where he grew up.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump trolls Trudeau as 'governor' of Canada
item.description: President-elect Trump mocked Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, calling him the "governor" of the "Great State of Canada.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Bill Belichick considering offer to be UNC Chapel Hill head football coach
item.description: CHAPEL HILL, N.C. (WGHP) — Bill Belichick is in negotiations to potentially become the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill's next head coach. InsideCarolina’s Grant Hughes first reported that Belichick interviewed for the job earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, NewsNation

item.title: Florida state lawmaker files measure eliminating tuition aid for undocumented immigrants
item.description: “President [Donald] Trump has made clear it is time to close the border and stop giving illegal immigrants rewards for breaking the law,” said Fine.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Biden stakes out economic legacy at Brookings as Trump readies takeover
item.description: President Biden on Tuesday made the case for the strength of his economic legacy as he prepared to leave office next month, arguing President-elect Trump would be inheriting a desirable situation and warning of the risks of the incoming administration’s proposals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Santa’s home is on Zillow, and you can take an AI-powered tour
item.description: You can take an AI-powered tour of Santa’s home, and you can even decorate your home like Santa with these festive décor ideas.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, KTLA 5

item.title: Greene says she is safe after bomb threat
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) confirmed she is safe after reports swirled of an email sent to law enforcement containing a bomb threat targeting the lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey drone sightings: Military analysts break down national security concerns, doubt hobbyists at play
item.description: Military drone experts tell Fox News that SUV-size drones over New Jersey are not likely hobbyists and a foreign adversary could be responsible.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Celebrities and coastal residents flee from wind-driven wildfire in Malibu
item.description: Thousands of Southern California residents are under evacuation orders and warnings as firefighters battle a wind-driven wildfire in Malibu.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Family of suspect speaks out after his arrest in UnitedHealthcare CEO's murder
item.description: The family of Luigi Mangione, the 26-year-old man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in New York City, has released a statement following his arrest on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Polygamous sect leader gets 50 years in prison in scheme to orchestrate sex involving children
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A polygamist religious leader who claimed more than 20 spiritual “wives” including 10 underage girls was sentenced to 50 years in prison on Monday for coercing girls as young as 9 years old to submit to criminal sex acts with him and other adults, and for scheming to kidnap them from
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about Luigi Mangione, the suspect arrested in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: A man arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson has been identified as Luigi Mangione, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, Fox 5

item.title: VSP charged two for hit-and-run crash that killed a North Carolina woman
item.description: Virginia State Police are investigating a deadly multi-vehicle accident on I-81 in Wythe County that killed a North Carolina woman and seriously injured two others on November 30.
item.dc:creator: Thad Randazzo, Fox 5

item.title: Passenger arrested after allegedly threatening crew member, attempting to divert Mexico flight to US
item.description: A Volaris flight in Mexico from Guanajuato to Tijuana was interrupted by an alleged hijacking attempt on Sunday morning by a passenger who wanted the plane diverted to San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, ABC 4

item.title: Enron's new CEO involved in 'Birds Aren't Real' conspiracy movement
item.description: Enron may be back, but its return appears to have a satirical angle. Despite promising to "fuel a bold vision of the future" and "unveil the most revolutionary technology the energy sector has ever seen," Enron announced its new CEO is not an experienced energy executive.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Supreme Court rejects appeal challenging Hawaii gun licensing requirements under Second Amendment
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court rejected a challenge to Hawaii's gun-licensing law on Monday, though three justices expressed a willingness to hear arguments over the issue later. The majority did not explain their reasoning in a brief order declining to take the case.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Veteran Daniel Penny found not guilty in NYC subway chokehold case over Jordan Neely's death
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Marine veteran who used a chokehold on an agitated subway rider was acquitted on Monday in a death that became a prism for differing views about public safety, valor and vigilantism.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Polygamist with 20 'wives' to be sentenced for sex acts with children
item.description: A polygamous religious leader who claimed more than 20 spiritual “wives ” including 10 underage girls faces decades in prison at his sentencing on Monday for coercing girls as young as 9 years old to submit to criminal sex acts with him and other adults.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Gas prices drop to lowest in 3 years, AAA says
item.description: Good news for the millions of drivers on American roads: gas prices are falling. The national average for a gallon of gas has hit a three-year low, the American Automobile Association (AAA) recently announced.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Police find bag carried by gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, say he likely fled NYC on bus
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said Friday. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4
